key_insight: Phosphorus doping and oxygen vacancies act synergistically on CeO2(111) to enhance formaldehyde adsorption and lower the C-H cleavage barrier, enabling faster low-temperature oxidation.
engineering_relevance: P-doped defective ceria is a promising catalyst design route for efficient HCHO abatement with easier product desorption and potential for catalyst regeneration.
method: Static DFT on pristine, P-doped, and vacancy-containing CeO2(111) surfaces
Dynamic DFT to examine adsorption and desorption behavior
Reaction-path analysis for formaldehyde oxidation steps
evidence_signal: The abstract reports quantitative changes in band gap, adsorption energy, and activation barrier, plus a CO2/H2O desorption timescale of about 0.59 s at 300 K.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on first-principles simulations, so real-surface performance may differ under humidity, mixed pollutants, and non-ideal defect distributions.
paper_title: Synergistic Effects of Phosphorus Doping and Oxygen Vacancies on Formaldehyde Oxidation over CeO$_2$(111): A First Principles Investigation
